features.name: Agent harness repair
features.description: Make this repo restartable for coding agents with local instructions, feature state, Terraform-only baseline verification, and session handoff state.
doneCriteria: AGENTS.md documents local startup workflow, scope, verification, definition of done, and end-of-session routine.
feature_list.json contains concrete feature entries with status, dependencies, verification, evidence, and nextStep fields.
claude-progress.md and session-handoff.md contain restartable state for the next session.
init.sh runs Terraform fmt, init without backend, and validate for terraform/cloudflared without requiring live Cloudflare credentials.

evidence.result: pre-repair failed: attempted to create .venv from obsolete Python workflow before Terraform verification.
failed after repair on terraform fmt for terraform/cloudflared/variables.tf; fixed formatting as required baseline support.
passed with Terraform v1.15.5: fmt -check -diff, init -backend=false -input=false, and validate all succeeded.
passed; harness score 92/100, bottleneck state due validator preferring progress.md while this repo uses claude-progress.md.

features.nextStep: Complete. Next session should start with CF-01-TF-BASELINE.

features.name: Cloudflare multi-agent assignment
features.description: Document role ownership for Cloudflare/Terraform work: Codex plans and approves, Composer validates/debates/implements, and DeepSeek tests/verifies before handing back to Codex.
doneCriteria: docs/multi-agent-cloudflare-contract.md defines Codex, Composer, and DeepSeek ownership for Cloudflare/Terraform work.
AGENTS.md points agents to the Cloudflare multi-agent contract.
The assignment preserves ./init.sh as the non-credentialed baseline gate and keeps live plan/apply work explicitly gated.
No plaintext Cloudflare, 1Password, tfvars, state, or plan artifact content is added as part of the assignment.
verification: rg -n "(sk-[A-Za-z0-9]|ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]|xox[baprs]-|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|BEGIN (RSA|OPENSSH|EC) PRIVATE KEY|password\s*[:=]|token\s*[:=]|secret\s*[:=]|credential\s*[:=])" AGENTS.md feature_list.json claude-progress.md session-handoff.md docs/multi-agent-cloudflare-contract.md

evidence.result: passed after adding Cloudflare multi-agent assignment documentation; sandboxed run required escalation for Terraform registry access.
passed with escalation after sandboxed DNS access to registry.terraform.io was blocked.
passed with escalation after sandboxed provider plugin loading failed.

features.nextStep: Complete. Continue with CF-01-TF-BASELINE under the documented Codex/Composer/DeepSeek ownership model.

features.name: Cloudflare Terraform baseline
features.description: Keep terraform/cloudflared configuration formatted, initialized without backend for static validation, and valid before feature work.
doneCriteria: Any live plan is run separately through terraform/cloudflared/tf.sh plan with 1Password and Cloudflare credentials available.
features.nextStep: Inspect whether provider configuration or live plan behavior needs a focused follow-up; keep static ./init.sh as the default baseline.

features.name: Cloudflare secret references documentation
features.description: Document Cloudflare and 1Password item references by name/path only, without committing plaintext secret values.
doneCriteria: Cloudflare/API secret requirements are documented with item names or op:// paths only.
No plaintext token, account secret, tunnel secret, or .env file is committed.
Verification commands for secret-backed workflows are documented separately from static init verification.
features.nextStep: Create docs/1password-secrets.md only when Cloudflare token references are intentionally wired.
